what_it_does: Provides the visual styling and layout rules for a full-screen, Vi-led project wizard UI, including the top bar, phase-specific stage layouts, interactive selection cards, a sequence editor, a deductions side panel, and AI-generation feedback states.
observations: Full-screen fixed wizard container with radial/linear gradient background and z-index 5.
Top bar uses glassmorphism (backdrop-filter blur) and contains back/close buttons, a brand mark, a phase tag, and a step rail.
Layout switches between .wizard__understand (single intake column) and .wizard__shape (main + 300px deductions panel) and collapses the panel below 880px.
Step indicator uses numbered circles with connecting lines and active/done states in a gold accent.
Vi-bubble component has an animated spinning avatar ring and a gradient core.
Concept step provides a large textarea with focus ring and example chips.
Direction step uses a 3-column grid of selectable cards with thumbnails, tick, tags, recommendation badge, and rationale line.
Renderer step uses a 3-column grid of selectable cards with icons and a suggested badge.
Sequence step shows a timeline/rail with editable state cards, an add button, and step labels.
Ready step presents a summary list and a Vi note.
Deductions side panel contains key/value rows, icon lists, and a voice button.
Thinking strip with animated blinking dots indicates AI generation.
Heavy reliance on CSS custom properties for colors, fonts, spacing, and radii, but no values are defined in this file.
Responsive behavior is handled by a single max-width media query at 880px.
Comments document design evolution, e.g., the brand mark replacing a macro-step strip and the restored two-phase adaptive init layout.
standouts: The stylesheet explicitly encodes a two-phase adaptive init layout (UNDERSTAND vs SHAPE) and a unified StreamRunner body.
Vi's presence is heavily styled: animated avatar ring, gold-accented bubble, thinking dots, and recommendation rationale tags.
The deductions side panel is treated as a first-class layout region with its own component styles.
AI-generation state gets dedicated UI (.wizard__thinking) with animated blinking dots.
conventions: Follows BEM-like naming with block__element and modifier classes such as .wizard__step and .wizard__step.done.
Uses CSS custom properties for theming but leaves them undefined in this file, relying on a global theme.
Uses a single max-width media query for responsive adaptation.
Uses comments to document design intent and phase semantics.
Breaks a pure utility approach by using component-specific classes with many property declarations.
.wizard__direction-more is declared twice with different declarations, creating a cascade dependency.
concerns: .wizard__direction-more is declared twice with different margin rules, which can produce unexpected cascade behavior.
Many custom properties are referenced but not defined in this file, so it cannot render correctly in isolation.
.wizard__seq-name:focus uses negative margins (-2px -4px) which may cause layout shifts.
Fixed positioning and z-index: 5 on .wizard may conflict with other overlays depending on the global stacking context.
.wizard__stage uses grid-template-columns: 1fr 320px without minmax on the first column, risking overflow before the media query applies.
notes: A polished, gold-accented CSS skin for a multi-step project wizard that couples layout, component states, and AI-feedback animations.
questions: What are the exact values of the referenced CSS custom properties (e.g., --ms-hairline, --accent-gold, --font-display)?
Is the StreamRunner component (.strm) styled elsewhere, or does this file assume its internal layout?
What is the intended behavior of the .wizard__seq-name negative-margin focus style across browsers?
Are the two .wizard__direction-more rules intentional progressive enhancement or accidental duplication?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose (Vi-led Project Wizard styling) matches the actual content: it styles a wizard with phases, steps, selections, sequence drafting, deductions, and AI thinking states.
apparent_themes: Warm, premium aesthetic (gold/bronze palette, glassmorphism, soft shadows)
Progressive disclosure via phased wizard steps
Transparency of AI reasoning (deductions panel, rationale tags)
intention_signals: It appears designed to make an AI-assisted project configuration feel like a friendly, trustworthy conversation with Vi—using gold-accented feedback, visible reasoning, and clear step-by-step progress—while keeping the interface responsive and visually polished.
novelty: The integration of an AI 'thinking' state, recommendation rationale tags, and a persistent deductions side panel directly into a wizard stylesheet, alongside phase-aware layout modes (UNDERSTAND/SHAPE).
contribution: A complete, theme-variable-driven stylesheet for a wizard UI that defines layout shells, step navigation, interactive selection cards, a sequence editor, a deductions panel, and AI-generation feedback animations; it contributes the visual grammar and responsive behavior for the project onboarding flow.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a CSS stylesheet that styles a full-screen, multi-step, Vi-led project wizard with a gold-accented warm theme, including top bar, phase layouts, selection cards, sequence editor, deductions side panel, and AI thinking animations.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to provide the visual layout, component states, and responsive styling for an AI-guided project creation wizard.
